nodes.label: Tests for mangaba backup and import commands.
Create a realistic ~/.mangaba directory structure for testing.
checkpoints/ is session-local trajectory cache — hash-keyed,         regenerated
backups/ is excluded so pre-update backups don't nest exponentially.
SQLite WAL/SHM/journal sidecars must not ship alongside the         safe-copied
Backup creates a valid zip containing expected files.
Backup does NOT include mangaba-agent/ directory.
Backup does NOT include PID files.
When no output path given, zip goes to ~/mangaba-backup-*.zip.
A zip containing state.db is accepted as a valid Mangaba backup.
A zip with only mangaba_state.db (old wrong name) is rejected.
A zip containing config.yaml is accepted (existing behaviour preserved).
Create a test zip with given files.
Import extracts files into mangaba home.
Import strips .mangaba/ prefix if all entries share it.
Import rejects a zip that doesn't look like a mangaba backup.
Import blocks zip entries with path traversal.
Import aborts when user says no to confirmation.
Import with --force skips confirmation and overwrites.
Import exits with error for nonexistent file.
Secret files must end up at 0600 after restore (zipfile drops mode bits).
Full round-trip: backup -> import to a new location -> verify.
Zip without mangaba markers fails validation.
No prefix when entries are at root.
Prefix detection returns empty for zip with only directory entries.
Backup exits when mangaba home doesn't exist.
When output path is a directory, zip is created inside it.
Output path without .zip gets suffix appended.
Backup handles empty mangaba home (no files to back up).
Backup skips files with pre-1980 timestamps (ZIP limitation).
Backup skips its own output zip if it's inside mangaba root.
Import handles EOFError during confirmation prompt.
Import handles KeyboardInterrupt during confirmation prompt.
Import handles permission errors during extraction.
Import auto-creates wrapper scripts for restored profiles.
Import doesn't create wrappers for profile dirs without config.
Import gracefully handles missing profiles module (fresh install).
Create a fake MANGABA_HOME with critical state files.
Pairing JSONs live outside state.db — snapshot must capture them         recursi
After restore, deleted pairing files reappear with original content.
An empty pairing directory should be silently skipped.
Tests for create_pre_update_backup — the auto-backup ``mangaba update``     runs
Pre-update backup should include the same user data that         ``mangaba backu
The ``backups/`` directory must be excluded so that each backup         doesn't
After more than ``keep`` backups are created, older ones are         pruned auto
Hand-dropped zips in ``backups/`` must not be touched by         rotation — it o
Mirror coverage: any value <1 should be floored, not literally         applied a
The floor preserves the new backup but should NOT regress the         rotation b
Tests for the ``_run_pre_update_backup`` wrapper in main.py —     covers config
--backup forces the pre-update backup for one run even when config is off.
With the default-off config and no --backup flag, the hook is silent         and
Users who explicitly set updates.pre_update_backup: true still get         a bac
Explicit pre_update_backup: false behaves the same as the default —         sile
--no-backup wins even when config says pre_update_backup: true.
Tests for create_pre_migration_backup — the auto-backup     ``mangaba claw migra
Pre-migration backup reuses the same exclusion rules as         ``mangaba backup
The zip produced by pre-migration backup must be a valid Mangaba         backup
Fresh install with no ~/.mangaba yet — nothing to back up.
Pre-migration rotation must only prune pre-migration-*.zip files,         leavin
